# Skills Catalog — knowledge

## decision
- [Skills Catalog and Skills Registry are separate products](/features/skills-catalog/knowledge/decision-skills-catalog-registry-separation.md) — Catalog (Kubeflow, marketplace/discovery) and registry (MLflow, workspace/governance) confirmed as separate products with different backends, shipped independently; catalog first.

## fact
- [NVIDIA skills ecosystem -- mature catalog, verification pipeline, complementary to MLflow](/features/skills-catalog/knowledge/fact-nvidia-skills-catalog-landscape.md) — NVIDIA has the most mature public skills catalog (2.7K stars, 35+ products, OMS signing, SkillSpector scanning, Skill Evaluator, Tier-3 evals, BENCHMARK.md, agentskills.io compliance, 5+ marketplace syndications); complements MLflow (trust/quality layer, not a registry). JFrog already partnered at GTC 2026.
- [Red Hat agentic skills (redhat.com/skills) -- fastest seed content for catalog](/features/skills-catalog/knowledge/fact-redhat-agentic-skills-seed-content.md) — Red Hat launched agentic skill packs at Summit 2026 for RHEL, OpenShift, and Ansible -- subscription-backed, live API-connected, available for Claude Code/Cursor/OpenShift Dev Spaces. Converting these to catalog entries is the minimum viable content path for RHAISTRAT-1940.
- [Skills Catalog 3.6 scope -- browse-only TP with pre-loaded content](/features/skills-catalog/knowledge/fact-skills-catalog-36-scope-recommendation.md) — Research-backed 3.6 scope recommendation -- read-only browse/search with 15-20 pre-loaded curated skills; installation automation and registry integration deferred to 3.7+; matches MCP Catalog DP-to-TP and Agent Catalog DP link-out precedents.
- [Skills Catalog direction -- Kubeflow, Git-backed, disconnected image](/features/skills-catalog/knowledge/fact-skills-catalog-direction.md) — Catalog direction established 2026-07-23 -- Kubeflow hub surface, GitHub repos as skill source, downstream image build for disconnected, metadata labels for grouping (no bundling), admin-manages/user-browses.
- [Skills Catalog -- overview and current status](/features/skills-catalog/knowledge/fact-skills-catalog-overview.md) — Skills Catalog is the AI Hub storefront for AI agent skills (Kubeflow surface, alongside MCP/agent catalogs); direction established 2026-07-23, pre-partition.
- [RHAISTRAT-1780 -- Skills Catalog scope and architecture](/features/skills-catalog/knowledge/fact-skills-catalog-rhaistrat-1780-scope.md) — RHAISTRAT-1780 Skills Catalog scope -- Go backend with PostgreSQL, odh-dashboard React/PatternFly 6 BFF UI module, trust tiers (RH/Partner/Org/Community), ConfigMap disconnected import, usage telemetry, 6-9 sprint effort estimate.
- [Skills registry/catalog competitive landscape (2026-07-21)](/features/skills-catalog/knowledge/fact-skills-competitive-landscape-2026.md) — Comprehensive competitive landscape -- cloud platforms (AWS AgentCore, Azure Agent 365, Google Gemini Enterprise, Databricks Unity AI), frameworks (Anthropic Marketplace ~600 skills, skills.sh 669K+, HF 40K+ with ARD spec, LangChain/LangSmith), security specialists (JFrog+NVIDIA, Cisco DefenseClaw, Credo AI, Zenity, TrueFoundry); SKILL.md is the standard (16+ tools), EU AI Act high-risk Aug 2 2026.
- [Pre-loaded skills (RHAISTRAT-1940) -- catalog empty-launch risk](/features/skills-catalog/knowledge/fact-skills-preloaded-content-risk.md) — RHAISTRAT-1940 (pre-loaded skills) is the top risk for catalog value -- without it the catalog launches empty; candidate content identified (starter kits, MCP configs, prompts, RH-authored skills) but owner/curation unresolved.
- [SkillsBench -- curated skills raise pass rates by 16.2pp; quality beats volume](/features/skills-catalog/knowledge/fact-skillsbench-quality-evidence.md) — SkillsBench (Stanford/CMU/Berkeley/Oxford, arXiv 2602.12670) scored 47,150 public skills -- average quality 6.2/12, 73% elevated safety risk; curated skills +16.2pp pass rate, self-generated -1.3pp; focused 2-3 module skills +18.6pp vs larger bundles +5.9pp.

## person
- [Aditi Saluja](/features/skills-catalog/knowledge/person-aditi-saluja.md) — Authored the comprehensive Skills Registry and Catalog status/scope/roadmap document; mapping upstream/RHOAI skills landscape + NVIDIA secure supply chain.

## question
- [Should the skills catalog extend kubeflow/hub or build a standalone service?](/features/skills-catalog/knowledge/question-skills-catalog-extend-hub-vs-new-service.md) — Architecture question -- extend kubeflow/hub as 4th catalog type (proven pattern, lower risk, same Go+PostgreSQL+BFF stack, MCP/agent catalog precedent) vs build standalone Go+PostgreSQL per RHAISTRAT-1780. Research recommends hub extension.
- [Should Red Hat model its skill catalog on NVIDIA's approach?](/features/skills-catalog/knowledge/question-skills-catalog-nvidia-collaboration.md) — Should RH build a GitHub-based skill catalog modeled on NVIDIA/skills? Integrate via federation? Use NVIDIA's validation/signing infrastructure? Strong "collaborate" signal from Peter/Ann Marie; Ramesh says pull NVIDIA data into our catalog.
- [Where do skill installation features live -- catalog, registry, or both?](/features/skills-catalog/knowledge/question-skills-installation-features-location.md) — Open question needing its own STRAT -- skill installation could live in catalog (marketplace.json, npx), registry (APM, LOLA, mlflow CLI), or both. Different flows for catalog-sourced vs registry-sourced skills.

## reference
- [agentskills.io -- Agent Skills specification (AAIF/Linux Foundation)](/features/skills-catalog/knowledge/ref-agentskills-io-spec.md) — The AAIF-governed Agent Skills specification -- defines SKILL.md directory structure, YAML frontmatter (name, description, license, compatibility, metadata, allowed-tools), progressive disclosure model; Apache 2.0, 170+ member orgs, 40+ supporting tools.
- [NVIDIA/skills -- verified agent skills catalog repo](/features/skills-catalog/knowledge/ref-nvidia-skills-repo.md) — NVIDIA's public skills catalog -- 2.7K stars, 35+ products (Physical AI, CUDA, RAG, robotics), OMS signing, skill cards, Tier-3 evals, BENCHMARK.md, npx distribution, daily sync from product repos, agentskills.io spec.
- [NVIDIA Verified Agent Skills -- trust pipeline deep-dive (Adel GDoc)](/features/skills-catalog/knowledge/ref-nvidia-verified-skills-trust-pipeline-gdoc.md) — Adel's deep-dive on NVIDIA's 6-stage trust pipeline for agent skills -- SkillSpector (68 vuln patterns, 17 categories), NVSkills-Eval (3-tier, benchmark scores per agent), Skill Card Generator (structured governance cards), OMS signing (cryptographic provenance). Informational reference, not necessarily our direction.
- [Skills Catalog vs. Skills Registry (GDoc, Ramesh Reddy)](/features/skills-catalog/knowledge/ref-skills-catalog-vs-registry-gdoc.md) — Ramesh's catalog vs registry comparison doc -- feature table, storage/identity/governance model, prioritization (Agent Registry first, lightweight Catalog, revisit full Registry), action items, stakeholder map, installation-location TODO.
- [Skills Registry & Catalog: Status, 3.6 Scope and Roadmap (GDoc, Aditi Saluja)](/features/skills-catalog/knowledge/ref-skills-status-scope-roadmap-gdoc.md) — Comprehensive status doc -- RFC-0008/0009 capability tables, RHAISTRAT-1780 catalog scope (Go/PostgreSQL/PF6), RHAISTRAT-1940 pre-loaded skills, 3-tier 3.6 shipping plan, MLflow vs NVIDIA gap analysis, NVIDIA verification pipeline deep dive, massive competitive landscape (AWS/Azure/Google/Databricks/Anthropic/skills.sh/HF/JFrog/Cisco/Credo AI/Zenity/TrueFoundry), market signals (SKILL.md standard, EU AI Act Aug 2 2026).
- [SkillsBench -- first quality benchmark for agent skills](/features/skills-catalog/knowledge/ref-skillsbench-benchmark.md) — SkillsBench (Stanford/CMU/Berkeley/Oxford) -- first systematic benchmark for agent skills quality; 47,150 skills scored, curated vs uncurated evidence, safety risk analysis; arXiv 2602.12670.
- [#forum-ai-asset-management Slack channel](/features/skills-catalog/knowledge/ref-slack-forum-ai-asset-management.md) — Slack channel for AI asset management discussions -- skills catalog/registry, agent catalog/registry, MCP catalog, packaging, cross-cutting asset governance.
- [Transcript: Priority Alignment -- AgentDev (2026-07-23)](/features/skills-catalog/knowledge/ref-transcript-priority-alignment-agentdev-2026-07-23.md) — AgentDev priority call -- skills vs agent registry prioritization, NVIDIA skills landscape, OCI/NPX/LOLA packaging, Plan B for MLflow upstream (Adel Zaalouk, Bill Murdock, Justin Sun, Peter Double, Aakanksha Duggal).
- [Transcript: Ramesh/Peter 1:1 -- Skills (2026-07-23)](/features/skills-catalog/knowledge/ref-transcript-ramesh-peter-skills-2026-07-23.md) — Ramesh/Peter 1:1 on skills direction -- catalog proposal in Kubeflow, governance belongs at agent level, NPX/Claude plugin support, OCI deferred.
- [Transcript: Skills Registry/Catalog (2026-07-23)](/features/skills-catalog/knowledge/ref-transcript-skills-registry-catalog-2026-07-23.md) — Skills Registry/Catalog meeting -- catalog/registry split confirmed, NVIDIA skills repo evaluation, namespace tenant model, catalog-to-registry integration, packaging plugins (Ann Marie Fred, Bill Murdock, Edson Tirelli, Justin Sun, Peter Double, Ramesh Reddy).
